title: Clean dist/ before every build so stale compiled artifacts never ship
context: tsc emits incrementally and never prunes dist/. After the brain->Hunch rename (hunchStore.ts), the pre-rename store/brainStore.js stayed in dist/ and was being included by the npm `files` allowlist — shipping dead, off-brand code in the published tarball. Discovered while making @hunch/cli publishable (npm pack dry-run showed both brainStore.js and hunchStore.js).
decision: `build` now runs `clean` first (node fs.rmSync dist, recursive+force) before tsc. `prepublishOnly` runs the clean build so `npm publish` can never ship a stale or renamed-away artifact. `files` narrowed to dist/**/*.js to also drop source maps.
consequences: Tarball dropped from 56 files/98kB to 29 files/64kB.
Any future file rename/delete in src/ can't leak its old compiled output into a publish.
Slightly slower builds (full recompile each time; project has no incremental/composite setup anyway).
alternatives_rejected: Add rimraf devDependency (avoided — used built-in node fs.rmSync to add no dep).
Leave dist uncleaned and rely on manual deletes (fragile; this is exactly how the brain artifact leaked).
